edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:c0c7a42f7a56
edges.original_sentence: He estimates that annual average productivity of fescue grassland is about 800 kg/ha, a figure that can be doubled or even tripled by conversion to brome grass (Bromus inermis), timothy (Phleum pratense), red fescue (Festuca rubra), and alfalfa (Medicago sativa).

source_spans.text: He estimates that annual average productivity of fescue grassland is about 800 kg/ha, a figure that can be doubled or even tripled by conversion to brome grass (Bromus inermis), timothy (Phleum pratense), red fescue (Festuca rubra), and alfalfa (Medicago sativa).

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:bfe1eacf1800
edges.original_sentence: Raking the donor fescue grassland helps reduce thatch and allows for thicker cuts of sod with greater root mass.

source_spans.text: Raking the donor fescue grassland helps reduce thatch and allows for thicker cuts of sod with greater root mass.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:bfe1eacf1800
edges.original_sentence: The raking also allowed us to cut deeper into the soil and gain a greater sod thickness and root mass.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:4ba0ea4a9ec9
edges.original_sentence: With time, the stability of initial species composition is altered and the plant community regresses to an earlier successional stage. This appears to be due to removal of root and rhizome masses and other below-ground parts deeper than five cm (19.5 in.) during sod cutting.

source_spans.text: With time, the stability of initial species composition is altered and the plant community regresses to an earlier successional stage. This appears to be due to removal of root and rhizome masses and other below-ground parts deeper than five cm (19.5 in.) during sod cutting.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:6f9f833f6b25
edges.original_sentence: Evidence for this is that transplanting clearly favored shallow-rooted species over deep-rooted species.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:3a02a64d4dc2
edges.original_sentence: Species diversity in the restored sites was greater than in the undisturbed grasslands.

source_spans.text: Species diversity in the restored sites was greater than in the undisturbed grasslands.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b8f7c4bd7ff5
edges.original_sentence: So far my observations indicate that fall mowing had little effect on species composition and abundance while mowing in the spring strongly favored wheatgrass and bluegrass species and disfavored rough fescue.

source_spans.text: So far my observations indicate that fall mowing had little effect on species composition and abundance while mowing in the spring strongly favored wheatgrass and bluegrass species and disfavored rough fescue.

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:b8f7c4bd7ff5
edges.original_sentence: Some herbs, such as goldenrod (Solidago spp.), bluebell (Campanula spp.), and vetch (Lathyrus spp.), also benefitted from mowing.

source_spans.text: Some herbs, such as goldenrod (Solidago spp.), bluebell (Campanula spp.), and vetch (Lathyrus spp.), also benefitted from mowing.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:bfe1eacf1800
edges.original_sentence: Spring green-up occurred approximately two weeks earlier on the resodded site than on comparable sites in the undisturbed control site and other fescue grasslands on similar ecological sites in Edgemont. This can be attributed to a greatly diminished thatch cover due to raking on the reconstructed sites, which allows more rapid warming of the soils.

source_spans.text: Spring green-up occurred approximately two weeks earlier on the resodded site than on comparable sites in the undisturbed control site and other fescue grasslands on similar ecological sites in Edgemont. This can be attributed to a greatly diminished thatch cover due to raking on the reconstructed sites, which allows more rapid warming of the soils.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:0e5bfd5ba6a7
edges.original_sentence: In support of this conclusion, I found that the plot on the restored site that I burned in the early spring greened-up even earlier, while sites mowed in the spring and covered with a layer of clippings greened-up slowly.

source_spans.text: In support of this conclusion, I found that the plot on the restored site that I burned in the early spring greened-up even earlier, while sites mowed in the spring and covered with a layer of clippings greened-up slowly.

source_document.title: Canada's Rough Fescue Grasslands: A Trial Restoration Project in Alberta is Yielding Encouraging Results
